citations.quote: Tenancies which cannot be private residential tenancies, including student accommodation provided by a university or other educational institution
Duty to make reasonable adjustments and failure to comply with that duty

findings.text: No tenancy or licence agreement was provided in the work order. The task cannot proceed to review without the document.
Student accommodation provided by a university may be excluded from private residential tenancy status under the Private Housing (Tenancies) (Scotland) Act 2016, schedule 1.
The Equality Act 2010 imposes a duty to make reasonable adjustments for disabled students in the provision of accommodation.
Without the agreement, it is uncertain whether the occupant holds a tenancy, licence, or other arrangement, and what terms apply to arrears.

proposed_artifacts.content:
# Task Limitation Note

## Task
Obtain and review the tenancy or licence agreement for napier-accommodation-arrears.

## Status
No agreement was provided in the work order. The task cannot be completed.

## Legal Context
- Student accommodation may be excluded from private residential tenancy under the Private Housing (Tenancies) (Scotland) Act 2016, schedule 1.
- The Equality Act 2010 requires reasonable adjustments for disabled students.

## Next Steps
A follow-up task is proposed to request the agreement from the client or relevant party.
proposed_artifacts.title: Task Limitation Note

proposed_tasks.instructions: Contact the client or relevant party to obtain a copy of the tenancy or licence agreement for the accommodation at issue. Specify that the document is needed to assess the legal basis of the arrears claim.
proposed_tasks.title: Request the tenancy or licence agreement from the client

risk_flags.text: Proceeding without the agreement risks mischaracterizing the legal relationship and applicable rights.
Failure to consider reasonable adjustments could lead to discrimination claims under the Equality Act 2010.
The task is stalled until the agreement is obtained; delay may affect limitation periods or procedural deadlines.

summary: No tenancy or licence agreement was supplied in the work order. The task cannot review a document that is absent. The legal framework for student accommodation in Scotland is outlined, but without the agreement, its terms, status, and compliance cannot be assessed. A follow-up task is proposed to obtain the agreement.

uncertainties.text: The existence, terms, and type of occupancy agreement are unknown.
Whether the occupant is a disabled student entitled to reasonable adjustments under the Equality Act 2010 is unknown.
The specific arrears claim and any applicable notice periods or procedural requirements cannot be assessed without the agreement.
